titleEn: "BERRY’s software has made huge steps forward"
titleZh: BERRY草莓采摘机器人软件实现巨大飞跃
summaryZh: 德国农业科技公司Organifarms在2026年GreenTech Amsterdam展会上展示了其自主草莓采摘机器人BERRY的最新版本，核心亮点是软件的重大升级。CTO Marian Bolz表示，硬件基本保持不变，但软件在适应性上取得巨大进步，机器人现在能处理摆动、移动的草莓及复杂簇生情况，从而提升采摘速度和商业可行性。现有客户可通过服务包自动获得软件更新。公司正借助战略伙伴EBZ集团拓展国际市场，目前聚焦荷兰和德国核心客户，机器人售价12万欧元，另需年度服务费。
translationZh:
德国农业科技公司Organifarms在2026年阿姆斯特丹绿色科技展上展示了其自主草莓采摘机器人BERRY的最新版本，核心进展是软件的重大更新。公司首席技术官兼联合创始人Marian Bolz以及Franz Wanner（Organifarms董事总经理兼EBZ集团首席战略官，EBZ于2025年收购了Organifarms多数股权）代表公司出席了展会。

Marian表示：“BERRY的硬件基本保持不变，但软件取得了巨大进步。我们现在对果实的悬挂方式适应性强得多。机器人现在可以处理摆动的草莓、移动的草莓以及复杂的簇生情况，而去年这些只能以有限的方式实现。”

**自适应采摘，速度更快**
软件改进解决了机器人草莓采摘的核心挑战之一：果实悬挂在冠层中的可变性。BERRY使用先进的传感器和摄像头评估成熟度，并在茎部采摘每颗草莓，直接将果实放入即售的托盘中。

该机器人可全天候运行，并在温室垄间系统中自主导航。Marian表示，适应性的提升直接关系到商业可行性。“显然，这些都是提高速度并使其成为可行商业案例所必需的条件。”

**自动更新包含在服务包中**
对于现有客户，软件改进会自动交付。“当你购买机器人时，软件不会一成不变。在我们的服务和维护包中，更新是包含在内的，因此无论何时你拥有系统，都能获得性能改进、可靠性改进和可用性改进，”Marian说。“系统不是静态的。硬件将是静态的，因为你不想在那方面出现中断，但软件会通过互联网自动更新。”

**多数股权收购与新领导层**
Marian表示，EBZ的收购打开了以前无法触及的大门。“有了EBZ集团这个强大的战略合作伙伴，我们现在有了新的机遇，特别是在技术规模化拓展和进入国际市场方面。”

Organifarms目前处于受控的推广阶段，有意限制销售范围，以便能够可靠地支持客户。“我们仍处于尚未全力扩张的阶段，并且谨慎避免将机器人销售到维护更困难的偏远地区。我们的重点是荷兰和德国的核心客户。”

BERRY需要在标准的温室垄间系统中运行，目前售价为12万欧元，另根据所选套餐收取年度服务维护费。
featuredReason: 该新闻聚焦草莓采摘机器人BERRY的软件重大升级，直接回应了智慧农业中机器人适应性、采摘速度与商业可行性的核心痛点。内容来自行业展会一线报道，包含CTO原声引述、技术细节（处理摆动/簇生果实）、商业模式（自动更新/服务包）及市场策略（受控推广/聚焦核心区域），对智慧畜牧与设施农业从业者具有高参考价值。

 Tuesday, 21 July 2026
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 Organifarms' Marian Bolz:

 "BERRY’s software has made huge steps forward"

 

 
 Organifarms showcased the latest iteration of BERRY, its autonomous strawberry harvesting robot, at GreenTech Amsterdam 2026, with a significant software update as the headline development. The German agritech company was represented by CTO and Co-Founder Marian Bolz and Franz Wanner, who serves as Managing Director of Organifarms and Chief Strategy Officer at EBZ Group, which acquired a majority stake in Organifarms in 2025."BERRY's hardware has stayed more or less the same, but the software has made huge steps forward," says Marian. "We are now much more adaptive to how the fruits are hanging. The robot can now deal with swinging strawberries, moving strawberries, and difficult cluster situations, which was only possible in a limited way last year."© Wesley Francis | HortiDaily.comFranz Wanner, Maren Barreiro, Theresa Westhoff and Marian Bolz from Organifarms 

 Adaptive harvesting at higher speedsThe software improvements address one of the core challenges of robotic strawberry harvesting: the variability of how fruit hangs within the canopy. BERRY uses advanced sensors and cameras to assess ripeness and harvest each strawberry at the stem, placing fruit directly into punnets ready for sale.

 The robot operates 24/7 and navigates autonomously through the greenhouse row system. Marian says the improved adaptability is directly tied to commercial viability. "Obviously, these are the things required to increase speeds and make this a viable business case."

 Automatic updates included in service packageFor existing customers, the software improvements are delivered automatically. "When you buy a robot, the software won't stay the same. Within our service and maintenance package, updates are included, so whenever you have a system, you also get performance improvements, reliability improvements, and usability improvements," Marian says.

 "The system is not static. The hardware will be static because you don't want interruptions on that end, but the software is updated automatically via the internet."

 A majority acquisition and new leadershipMarian says the EBZ acquisition opens doors that were previously out of reach. "With the EBZ Group as a strong strategic partner, we now have new opportunities, particularly in scaling our technology and accessing international markets."

 Organifarms is currently in a controlled rollout phase, deliberately limiting sales to markets where it can reliably support customers. "We are still in a phase where we are not scaling with full force, and we are careful not to sell robots to very remote locations where maintenance is more challenging. Our focus is on core customers in the Netherlands and Germany."BERRY requires a standard greenhouse row system to operate and currently costs €120,000, with an additional yearly service and maintenance fee depending on the package chosen.For more information:OrganifarmsMarian Bolz, CTO and Co-Founder[email protected]www.organifarms.de
